document.masterDocTitle: Taxonomic review of the genus Morabia Hausmann & Tujuba, 2020 with descriptions of two new species and introducing five new generic combinations (Lepidoptera, Geometridae, Ennominae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Taxonomic review of the genus Morabia Hausmann & Tujuba, 2020 with descriptions of two new species and introducing five new generic combinations (Lepidoptera, Geometridae, Ennominae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: The African Natural History Research Trust (ANHRT), Street Court Leominster-Kingsland, HR 6 9 QA, United Kingdom. gyulalaszlo @ anhrt. org. uk; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0001 - 9862 - 8290
SNSB-Zoologische Staatssammlung München, Münchhausenstr. 21, Munich, Germany.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
Tujuba et al. (2020)
: 74. Holotype , female, Kenya ,
Sokoke Forest
, coll. ZSM .

Holotype .
Female, “
Kenya
, Sokoke Forest , 31.7.94, leg. Dr. Politzar ”, BOLD sample ID: BC ZSM Lep add 57894 ( ZSM ).

subSubSection.paragraph:
Paratypes . DRC .
1 female , E.[lizabeth]ville [ Lubumbashi ],
16.x.1955
, Seydel , gen. slide No. : ZSM G 20935 ( ZSM )
Kenya .
1 female , with the same data as in the holotype
;
1 female , same locality,
2.vii.1994
, BOLD sample ID: BC ZSM
Lep 74389
;
1 male , Watama ,
2.viii.1973
, leg. H. Politzar , gen. slide No. : ZSM G 20934 ( ZSM )
.
Tanzania .
3 females , Bagamoyo dist. Vigwaza ,
231 m
, 06°42.89’S , 038°52.52’E ,
30.iii.2014
, leg. Ph. Darge
;
1 female , Pwani region , Ruvu forest reserve,
220 m
, 06°57.27’S , 038°49.36’E ,
03.iii.2014
, leg. Ph. Darge ( ZSM )
.

Additional material examined.
Kenya .
1 male , Gedi , Arabuko , Sokoke Forest Reserve , Brachystegia sand forest, ca.
50m
, 3º24’S , 39 º53’E ,
21.vii.2002
, leg. H.S. Staude ( RCHS )
.
Mozambique .
1 male , Maputo Special Reserve , West Gate , Sand Forest ,
22m
, 26°30’14.2”S , 32°42’59.6”E ,
3-13.xii.2016
, Light Trap , Aristophanous, M. , Cristovão, J. , László, G. , Miles, W. leg., ANHRT
:
2017.22, unique number: ANHRTUK00011745, gen. slide No. : LG 5467 ( ANHRT )
.
Republic of South Africa .
1 male , Natal , Pinetown , Krantzkloof Nature Reserve ,
200m
, 29°46’S , 30°50’E , Riverine forest , H.S. Staude ,
26.iv.1995
, gen. slide No. : LG 5527
;
1 male , Northern Province , Gundani village , Brachystegia woodland,
840m
, 22.39°S , 30.34°E ,
26.xi.2000
, H.S. Staude , gen. slide No. : LG 5528 ( RCHS )
;
1 female , The Haven , Transkei ,
2.i.1981
, leg. A.J. Duke , gen. slide No. : NHMUK 010317360 (prepared by László ) ( NHMUK ).

Diagnosis.
Morabia politzari (Hausmann & Tujuba, 2020 )
has conspicuous, large, isolated, black subapical blotches on the underside of fore- and hindwings, similar to
M. nigripunctata ( Warren, 1897 )
and
M. cryptica
sp. n.
The distinctive characters between the three taxa are discussed in detail under the diagnosis of the last species.

Description. Detailed description of the species is provided in
Tujuba et al. (2020)
.

Genetic information ( Fig. 44 ). BIN: BOLD: ABX0432 (n=2 from Kenya ), intraspecific variation 0.25%. Nearest neighbouring species:
M. smithi
, BIN-sharing at a genetic distance (minimum pairwise distance) of 1.1%. Minimum genetic distances of less than 2% in allopatric populations (
M. smithi
versus
M. politzari
) are often best classified as different subspecies, but in this case the marked differences in morphology clearly support distinct species status of both taxa.

Distribution ( Fig. 43 ). The species is distributed along the eastern coast of Africa with records from DRC , Kenya , Tanzania , Mozambique and South Africa . The specimens from Mozambique and South Africa represent new country records.
